data.title: Labor market 'solid,' fight against inflation is making progress, SF Fed's Daly says
data.description:
"I really think the labor market is in solid shape," San Francisco Fed President Mary C. Daly said on Thursday in discussing the central bank's two mandates of full employment and price stability.

"It may take a little longer to find a job" than when the economy was "frothy," but workers are generally getting the jobs they want, she said.

As for the Fed's 2% inflation target, "I can't guarantee that we'll get there this year," she said, adding that it's not in her forecast.

"We are making progress," Daly said. "More importantly, we are resolute in getting the job done."

Note, though, that the Fed has no number to define full employment. At last check, the unemployment rate stood at 4.2% in April. As for inflation, the March PCE price index rose 2.6% Y/Y.

During many periods, the Fed's two mandates are not in conflict. But when they are, then "you have to balance the risks," she said. "You're not just balancing today, but over time" because monetary policy works with a lag.

When asked about Moody's downgrade of the U.S. sovereign credit rating, the Federal Reserve doesn't make fiscal policy, Daly said. Rather, it makes monetary policy. She declined to say whether the downgrade makes her job any harder. She's focused on stable prices and full employment.

Every new administration coming into the White has a list of policies they want to implement, Daly said. When a new administration comes in, she looks at three things:

  • How do the proposals get amended, changed or modified?
  • What is the end-state of the proposals? What is the scope magnitude, and timing of the end-stat?.
  • What's the net effect of the resulting government action?

Right now, businesses are pausing as they assess the effects of new policies, she said.  As a result, the Fed is in a wait-and-see model

"We're in a good place right now" in terms of monetary policy, she said. Note that the federal funds rate target range now sits at 4.25%-4.50%.

Independence of the Federal Reserve "is essential." Therefore, she doesn't make political comments. 

Discussing the attacks by the Trump administration on the Fed's independence, Daly said it's not the first time that the Federal Reserve has been asked by an administration to take certain actions. Criticism from elected officials "is part of the job," she said. However, it's important that the Fed doesn't let political considerations affect its monetary policy decisions.  

The Fed is accountable to the U.S. Congress, but also to the American people, she said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Bowen Acquisition receives NASDAQ’s notification of non-compliance
data.description:
• Bowen Acquisition Corp (NASDAQ:BOWN) said on Thursday that it has received a letter from the Nasdaq’s listing department advising the company that it does not comply with Nasdaq’s listing rule for continued listing because NASDAQ has not received the company’s quarterly report on Form 10-Q. 
  • NASDAQ has informed the company that it has until July 28, 2025, to submit a plan to regain compliance regarding this delinquent report. 
  • The company said it is working diligently to complete the Form 10-Q.

data.title: Femasys announces public offering, concurrent private placement
data.description:
• Femasys (NASDAQ:FEMY) plans to sell common stock in an underwritten public offering. A 30-day option will be granted to underwriters to purchase up to 15% additional shares.
  • All securities in both offerings will be sold by Femasys.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Allied Gaming & Entertainment receives expected Nasdaq notice regarding delayed quarterly report
data.description:
• Allied Gaming & Entertainment (NASDAQ:AGAE) on Thursday said it has received a deficiency letter from Nasdaq, notifying the company that it is not in compliance with the requirements for continued listing, as the company’s Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the quarter ended March 31, 2025 was not filed with the SEC by the required due date of May 15, 2025.
  • The company also remains delinquent in filing its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024.
  • Under Nasdaq rules, the company has until June 16, 2025, to submit a plan to regain compliance.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Rush Enterprises increases stock repurchase program to $200M
data.description:
• Rush Enterprises (NASDAQ:RUSHA) boosts its share repurchase program by $50M, raising the total authorization to $200M.
  • The increase follows the company nearing its original $150M buyback limit.
  • The program runs through December 31, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Dell Technologies pops as IT giant raises full-year forecast amid strong AI demand
data.description:
Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL) shares rose 3.5% in extended trading on Thursday after the IT giant reported fiscal first-quarter results and raised its guidance for the full-year, due to strong demand for artificial intelligence products.

For all of fiscal 2026, Dell said it now sees adjusted earnings of $9.40 per share, up $0.10 from its prior outlook and above the $9.21 per share estimate. Sales for the full-year are expected to be between $101B and $105B, compared to the $103.11B estimate.

Second-quarter adjusted earnings are expected to be $2.25 per share, while sales are forecast to be between $28.5B and $29.5B. Analysts were anticipating earnings of $2.11 per share on $25.33B in revenue.

The strong guidance comes after Dell said it earned an adjusted $1.55 per share on $23.38B in revenue, for its fiscal first-quarter, which ended on May 2. Of the company's two units, Client Solutions Group generated $12.5B in revenue, up 5% year-over-year. Dell's Infrastructure Solutions Group delivered $10.3B in revenue for the quarter, up 12% year-over-year. Included in that was an 16% rise in servers and networking revenue at $6.3B, a new company record.

A consensus of analysts expected the company to earn an adjusted $1.70 per share on $23.18B in sales.

“We achieved first-quarter record servers and networking revenue of $6.3 billion, and we’re experiencing unprecedented demand for our AI-optimized servers,” said Jeff Clarke, vice chairman and chief operating officer, Dell Technologies. “We generated $12.1 billion in AI orders this quarter alone, surpassing the entirety of shipments in all of FY25 and leaving us with $14.4 billion in backlog.”

The company will host a conference call at 4:30 p.m. EST to discuss the results.

data.title: Universal Non-GAAP EPS of $0.80, revenue of $702.28M
data.description:
• Universal press release (NYSE:UVV): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.80.
  • Revenue of $702.28M (-8.9% Y/Y).
  • Outlook
    • Flue-cured and burley tobacco crops grown outside of China are expected to increase by 20% and 30%, respectively, in fiscal year 2026.
    • Crop purchases in Brazil in fiscal year 2026 are not following the accelerated patterns seen in fiscal year 2025.

data.title: Wall Street ekes out gain, bonds rally after weak GDP data
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages on Thursday ended with marginal gains, amid a bond rally sparked by weak GDP data and more back-and-forth on tariffs. 

The benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) erased an advance of nearly 1%. That climb was driven by Nvidia's (NVDA) blowout earnings report and a ruling by a U.S. court that struck down most of President Donald Trump's April tariffs. A federal appeals court later allowed the tariffs to stay in effect. The S&P seesawed through the session, eventually closing at +0.4%.

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) finished +0.4%, after shedding an intraday gain of 1.5%. The Dow (DJI) ended +0.3%, as a post-earnings slide in Salesforce (CRM) capped gains.

Over in the bond market, U.S. Treasury yields were lower as traders snapped up bonds. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 4 basis points to 4.44%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) also fell 5 basis points to 3.95%.

"Today was another one of those days that shows just how headline-driven the market has become. Even UBS came out, making the case that this will remain a volatile market, as everyone is looking for trade and fiscal headlines, which is a fancy way of saying ‘We have no idea what will happen next,’” Leo Nelissen, part of investing group iREIT+HOYA Capital, told Seeking Alpha.

“In this market, I stick to my strategy, which is building a cash position after having bought the dip in April,” Nelissen added. “If we get more reversals like the one today, I’m putting more money to work."

Before the opening bell, the Bureau of Economic Analysis revised its estimate of U.S. Q1 GDP growth to a decrease of 0.2% from a contraction of 0.3%. 

The GDP print helped bump up Federal Reserve rate cut expectations. 

Earlier, U.S. stock index futures had kicked off the day with a big gain. The advance had come after a federal trade court on Wednesday struck down the “reciprocal” tariffs that Trump imposed on what he called “Liberation Day,” putting an immediate stop to the collection of a sweeping set of levies that the President imposed. But later in the afternoon, a brief order granting a stay on the decision was issued by the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit,  

Meanwhile, AI darling Nvidia (NVDA) rose 3.2% after the firm reported fiscal first-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations, despite the impact of added export control curbs to China.

“Nvidia continues to defy expectations, proving it’s still powering the AI wave. Nvidia's blowout earnings reinforce the idea that innovation can still thrive even in a market full of uncertainty,” said Paul Stanley, CIO at Granite Bay Wealth Management.

Salesforce (CRM) closed -3.1% despite a strong Q1 earnings result. RBC Capital downgraded the stock to "sector perform" from "outperform" due to “execution risk” tied to the planned acquisition of Informatica. See more here.

data.title: ScanTech AI receives Nasdaq notices for filing delay, market value non-compliance
data.description:
• ScanTech AI Systems (NASDAQ:STAI) received two Nasdaq notices for non-compliance with listing requirements.
  • The first notice, dated May 22, 2025, relates to the late filing of its Q1 2025 report, the company has 60 days to submit a compliance plan.
  • If accepted, Nasdaq may grant up to 180 days, until Nov. 17, 2025 to regain compliance with filing requirements.
  • The second notice, received May 27, 2025, concerns failure to meet the $50M minimum market value requirement.
  • ScanTech has 180 days, until Nov. 24, 2025 to regain compliance by maintaining a $50M market value for 10 consecutive business days.
  • Source: Press release
